Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Exactly. Cats seem to know that sound is the medium of communication with humans.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: Do wild cats know that?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Probably not. I read that they rarely meow, unlike house cats.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: They’ve really evolved.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: What do you like to do in your free time?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Well, I really enjoy swimming, especially in the ocean.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: Really? Me, too. I love swimming in saltwater.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Cool! I guess we’re both really lucky to live near the ocean.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: Yes, we are. But you need to be careful when you swim in the ocean around here. People have spotted sharks recently.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Oh, thanks for letting me know. I haven’t heard about that.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: I just read a news article about it. It said that sharks have a very strong sense of smell.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: So, what you mean is that… I should take a shower before swimming in the ocean?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: Haha. Don’t be silly. What I’m saying is that you shouldn’t go in the water if you have an open wound. Sharks are very sensitive to the smell of blood.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: I see. I’ll keep that in mind.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Oh, what are these little pink things behind the glass?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: They’re hermit crabs. My friend keeps them as pets, and she gave me some.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: They’re really cool. Why are their shells pink?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: There’s no special reason. They can’t grow hard shells like sea turtles, so I gave them some pink shells to move into.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: I see. So do you have to give them shells every now and then?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: Yes. I prefer colorful ones. What’s interesting is that hermit crabs are really social.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Really? Do you mean like ants and bees?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: Kind of. What I’m saying is that they live in groups and help each other. Sometimes they even trade shells with each other.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: That’s cool. I wouldn’t mind raising some myself.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: Hey, have you heard about the high school cartoon character contest?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Yeah. But I don’t know the details. Do you know what the theme is?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: It’s endangered species around the world.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Interesting. You should enter it. You’re super good at drawing.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: I am. I’m going to draw a red panda.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Those are really cute. But are they endangered?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: Yes, they’re seriously endangered. In fact, there are fewer than 10,000 left in the world.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Oh, no! They need to be protected. It would be a sad world without them.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M1
Script: Nelson, how do you feel?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M2
Script: I’m feeling much better now. Thanks, Dad.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M1
Script: I was worried we might have to cancel our trip. The island doesn’t have many facilities if you get sick.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M2
Script: I didn’t mean to worry you.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M1
Script: I know. They just announced that we’ll arrive at the island in about half an hour.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M2
Script: Great. I’ll be back to normal once we get off this old boat.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M1
Script: Are you sure? We have a lot to do before it gets dark.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M2
Script: Of course. I’ll set up the tent if you start the cooking fire for dinner.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M1
Script: Great! Now that you’re feeling better, I’m getting excited for the trip again.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M2
Script: I’m really looking forward to camping on the beach too!

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: Hi, Rosie. You’ve been to Singapore before, haven’t you?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Yes, I went there early last year. Why?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: My family is considering a short trip there this summer, so I was wondering if you have any recommendations or travel tips.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Really? You’re going to love it. You’ve got to check out the Gardens by the Bay.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: Yeah, I already have that on my list. I can’t wait to go there.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: As far as tips, remember to check out the laws. For example, you can’t chew gum in public. There’s a fine for that.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: What? Are you serious?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Yes, I am. Also, you can get a fine for eating on buses and trains.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: Thanks for letting me know!

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Thank you for calling Hotel Aurora. How may I help you?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: Hello, I want to double-check the reservation I made online.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: I can help you with that. Can I have your name, please?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: Andrew Lim.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Thank you, Mr. Lim. You’re booked from July 27 to 30 for four guests. Is this correct?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: Yes. It’s a pet-friendly room with two double beds, correct? We’re bringing our dog.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Please remember to show your dog’s shot records when you arrive. Your dog lead must be no longer than two meters.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: Got it. Thank you for your help.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: No problem. We look forward to your stay.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Have you ever heard of a treehouse hotel?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: No, I haven’t. Are you saying that a hotel is up in a tree?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Yes, the hotel rooms are literally in a tree. There are quite a few of them in Africa and Europe.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: Cool! So it’s like sleeping in the middle of a forest.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Right. I’m going to book one of those hotels during my trip to Africa next month. I’m really looking forward to sleeping in a tree.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: I can’t wait to hear about it. By the way, have you heard of the ice hotel in Sweden?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Ice? Like in the movie Frozen?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: Yeah. The hotel has ice beds in the rooms. Can you imagine that?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: No. But it would be just as cool as sleeping in a tree. You could also see the Northern Lights there!

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Ben, are you done packing your bag for the trip?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: Yes, Mom. I think I’ve packed everything.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Let’s check. Did you pack your toothbrush?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: Oh, no! I forgot that. Thanks for reminding me.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: What about your socks?

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: I have them in my bag. I also have a bag to bring back my dirty clothes in.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: Good. And remember to pack your medicine.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: M
Script: I already have it. I think I should take extra batteries too, just in case.

Author: Hong
Label: H1
Speaker: W
Script: That’s a good idea. It’s better to be safe than sorry.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Jamie! How was your summer?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Hi, Eddie. It was great! I went swimming, played basketball, and watched football games almost every day.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: That sounds fun! I didn’t know you were so into sports.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: I’m interested in all kinds of sports, but basketball is my favorite. What about you?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: I’m not really into sports, but I’m interested in photography. I take photos of everyday life, like city streets and food.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Hey, do you think you can take photos of my basketball team someday?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Sure! That could give me practice taking action photos.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Definitely. We have a practice this weekend.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: I’ll be there.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Welcome to our show, Meet New People! Today, I’d like to introduce a special guest to you: Mr. Roberto, who is an art restorer in Italy. Hello, Mr. Roberto!

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Hello. Thank you for inviting me today.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: My pleasure. Mr. Roberto, would you be able to tell us about your job?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Sure, I restore fine artworks, like old paintings and sculptures.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: How old are the artworks usually?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: It varies, but a recent work I worked on was a painting of an Italian artist from the 1500s.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: That’s amazing. What kind of skills do you need to become an art restorer?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: It requires diverse skills, but most importantly, you’re expected to have a scientific background to develop the restoration process and knowledge of history to understand the artwork.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Science and history. Two key school subjects!

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Mom, can you help me?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Of course. What do you need help with?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: I need to write a short report on a particular job. So I was thinking about writing about your job, medical social worker. Can you tell me what you do?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Sure. I help patients and their families deal with stress.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: When do they need your help the most?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: They often need emotional help after they have a big surgery or accident.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: What do you need to do to become a medical social worker?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: You need to have a college degree in social work or psychology and have relevant training. Also, you’re expected to be a good listener.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: You must have worked really hard.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Hey, what are you up to?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: I am filling out an application form for a mentoring program.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Why do you want to get a mentor?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: I am interested in beautiful buildings, so I want to study architecture in college. But I don’t know if I should study architectural design or architectural engineering. They are two different fields.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Ah, so you’d like to talk to a mentor to help you decide what to study?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Yes. I hope they can help me figure out which field is better for me.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: I need some advice for a similar problem. Maybe I should get a mentor too.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Good idea. I can send you the link to apply.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Thanks for calling the Media Arts Museum.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Hello, I’m calling to ask some questions about the museum.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: I’d be glad to answer them.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Thanks. I heard you show rare classic movies, but I can’t find the schedule on your website.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: We are updating the schedule now, and it will be posted online tomorrow.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: That’s good. I’ll check it out later. I also saw that you offer a computer art class. Is it still open?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Yes, it is. We also have a beginner photography class. There are spots available, but you’re expected to bring your own camera.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: I see. Thank you for the information.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Is this Hwaseong Fortress?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Yes, Ravi. It’s a UNESCO World Heritage Site. King Jeongjo of the Joseon dynasty built it to honor his father.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Wow, he must have really loved his father.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Well, he actually lost his father when he was just eleven, so he probably really missed his father.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: How sad. It reminds me of the Taj Mahal in India.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: The Taj Mahal? Why?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Emperor Shah Jahan built it to honor his wife, who died while giving birth to their child.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Wow, I didn’t know that. That’s quite a story.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: It sure is.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: How was your trip to Korea?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: It was amazing! When I was there, I saw a really cool traditional dance performance by masked dancers.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: That does sound really cool. What were the masks made of?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: They were made of wood and painted with bright colors. Each mask represented a different character.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: It reminds me of the Venice Carnival in Italy.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Exactly. They both involve wearing masks, but the cultural significance is quite different.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: In what way?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: The Korean mask dance explores social issues through song and movement, whereas the Venice Carnival is more of a celebration of art and culture.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: I see. I would love to see the Korean mask dance sometime.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Hi, Regina. Are you enjoying your stay here in Seoul?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Yes. The city is so exciting but coming from Kuwait, I’m surprised that there are so many mountains in Seoul.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: It’s one of the many highlights of Seoul.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: It sure is.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: What’s also nice is that most of the mountains have hiking trails, so you can enjoy a great view of the city while hiking.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Wow! I’ll put hiking on my to-do list during my stay here.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: I can take you on a hike if you want.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Thanks! My country is mostly flat, so it’d be greatto experience a hike in the mountains.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: So, Paula, we’re finally visiting Gangnam today.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Great! Should we take the subway or the bus?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: The bus. There’s a bus stop right there.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Wow, look at the digital display!

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Yes. It has information about the buses that stop here. We should take bus number 9408. It’s arriving in three minutes and there are seats available.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Oh, really? How do you know?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Look at the display. It shows the estimated wait time and available seats.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Cool! I knew Korea was high-tech, but I’m surprised that their bus stops are so advanced. It’s super impressive!

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Yes, for sure. It makes getting around really convenient.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Good morning, Sarah. We’re excited to hear about the mug you designed for K-drama fans. What inspired you to create it?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Most people love to drink something while watching TV. So I wanted to design a special mug for fellow fans.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Great. Tell us about the design.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: The mug is decorated with a famous scene from a popular K-drama.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: I see. And the handle looks quite special. Can you tell us more about it?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Sure. It’s inspired by goreum, a hanbok bow.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: What a wonderful combination of tradition and creativity!

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Thanks. I hope K-drama fans will enjoy my mug while watching K-dramas or when they just want to relax.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Hey, did you enjoy the tour of Seoul yesterday?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Yes, I had a great time exploring Seoul with my friend.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Cool. Did anything in Seoul surprise you?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Yes. I am surprised that there are so many interesting cafés here.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Oh, did you go to one?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Yeah, we went to a geomungo café.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Geomungo? You mean the traditional Korean string instrument?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Yes. You can have coffee and learn about geomungo there. The owner of the café even gives geomungo lessons to the customers.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Cool!

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: The café also sells books about geomungo and other items related to the instrument.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Nice! I would love to visit that café sometime.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Hi, Minji. I’m glad I ran into you. I wanted to ask you something.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Sure. What is it?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: I just read something about a game called yutnori. What exactly is it?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Oh, it’s a Korean board game that we play in teams with our family and relatives on traditional holidays.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Oh, cool. How do you play it?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: You throw four sticks made of wood, called yut, and move your pieces based on your throw. It’s a game of strategy and luck.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Interesting! It kind of reminds me of a game called pachisi in India. It uses sticks too, so the concept is similar.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: It’s pretty cool how games can be so similar across cultures.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Yes, indeed.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Hey, Mina. Look at this painting! She looks like she’s feeling blue.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Feeling blue? Does that mean she’s sad?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Yeah. I think blue is often associated with sadness.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: That makes sense because a couple of my friends talked about the “Monday blues” after a fun weekend. I wasn’t quite sure what that meant, but it likely means they were sad.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Expressions like that make it hard to learn English.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Definitely. Do you know of any other colors that mean different emotions?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Yeah. You can describe someone as being “green with envy.”

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Interesting. I’d like to learn more about colors and emotions in English.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Mrs. Roberts, have you given any thought to how we should decorate the art room?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Yes, Mr. Cheng. I watched a TV show on colors and got some ideas.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Great! What do you suggest?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: I think we can use different colors to affect the mood of the room. For example, we can use cool colors like green and light blue to create a calm mood.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: That’s a good idea.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Another direction we could go is to use warm colors like yellow and orange.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: How do warm colors affect mood?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: They can create a sense of energy. Energetic feelings could stimulate students’ imaginations.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: I’ll talk about your ideas with other teachers in the next meeting. I like the idea of warm colors myself.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: Mrs. Seo, I have a question about the last lesson.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: What is it?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: I noticed that Korean bills have a historical figureon the front and a painting on the back. Are the front and back related?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Yes. The historical figure on the front is connected to the art on the back.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: That’s interesting. So on the 5,000 won bill, what does the painting on the back have to do with the man on the front?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: The man is Yi I, and the painting was done by his mother.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: She was a great artist! The plants look so real.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Many of my students have similar comments.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: The house on the bill is beautiful, too. I wonder why it is drawn there.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: It’s where he grew up. The mother and son are honored by the bill’s design.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: When you said I would be impressed with this church, you were right. Look at the blue ceiling. It’s beautiful!

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: It really is. I’ve been wanting to bring you here since you arrived.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: What did they use to make such a unique shade of blue?

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: The builders used a rare stone that was only found in mines in the Middle East. It was even considered more valuable than gold at one time.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: I understand why the builders chose a stone like that, but it must have been expensive.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Exactly. I think coloring ceilings with this blue was seen as a way to create a sense of awe.

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: M
Script: It really is amazing. I feel like there’s a starry sky inside the church!

Author: Hong
Label: H2
Speaker: W
Script: Yes, I know what you mean.
